name: eflips-model

metadata.name: eflips-model
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Affero General Public License v3 or later (AGPLv3+)
metadata.summary: A common data model for the eflips family of electric vehicle simulation & optimization tools.

documentation:
[![Unit Tests](https://github.com/mpm-tu-berlin/eflips-model/actions/workflows/unittests.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/mpm-tu-berlin/eflips-model/actions/workflows/unittests.yml) 
[![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black)

# eflips-model

---

Part of the [eFLIPS/simBA](https://github.com/stars/ludgerheide/lists/ebus2030) list of projects.

---


This repository contains both the *reference specification*, an *SQLALchemy implementation* and a *usage example* of the eFLIPS database.

## Reference Specification

The reference specification is located (for now) in the huge PDF file [`schema.pdf`](schema.pdf). It is a UML diagram of the database schema. The diagram was created using [OmniGraffle](https://www.omnigroup.com/omnigraffle) (proprietary).

## SQLAlchemy Implementation

The SQLAlchemy implementation is located in the `eflips/model` directory. It is a Python package that contains the SQLAlchemy models and the database migration scripts. The package is structured as follows:

### Installation

Releases of the package will be made available on [https://pypi.org/](https://pypi.org/project/eflips-model/). As such, it is installable with `pip install eflips-model`. However, it should be used by including it in other packages as a dependency. The versioning scheme is [semantic versioning](https://semver.org/). This means that:

- patch releases (e.g. `1.0.0` to `1.0.1`) are backwards compatible bug fixes, without schama changes
- minor releases (e.g. `1.0.0` to `1.1.0`) are backwards compatible feature additions, with the schema changes being optional
- major releases (e.g. `1.0.0` to `2.0.0`) are backwards incompatible changes, with the schema changes being mandatory

Supported database backends are

- [PostgreSQL](https://www.postgresql.org) with the [PostGIS](https://postgis.net/) extension (`CREATE EXTENSION postgis;`)
  and `btree_gist` (`CREATE EXTENSION btree_gist;`)

### Usage

This package is not expected to be used directly. It is a dependency of the `eflips-*` packages.

This package utilizes GIS extensions through [GeoAlchemy](https://geoalchemy-2.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html).
However, we are not handling geometry on the python side in any special way. When developing a paclage that uses `eflips-model`, you will probably additionally
need [Shapely](https://shapely.readthedocs.io/en/stable/manual.html)
and [pyProj](https://pyproj4.github.io/pyproj/stable/), which are not pure python packages and require additional
dependencies to be installed on the system.

#### Schema updates

The schema updates are handled by [Alembic](https://alembic.sqlalchemy.org/en/latest/). The migration scripts are located in the `eflips/model/migrations` directory. To create a new migration script, execute the following commands in the root directory of the repository:

```bash
cd eflips/model
alembic revision --autogenerate -m "vx.y.z"
# Edit the migration script, as necessary
```

**Creating a migration script is required for every change to the database schema, which should also correspond to a minor or major version change in the package version.**

To apply the migration scripts, execute the following command in the root directory of the repository:

```bash
cd eflips/model
export DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@hostname:port/dbname # Change to your database URL
alembic upgrade head
```

### Testing

We use [pytest](https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/) for testing. The tests are located in the `tests` directory. To run the tests, execute the following command in the root directory of the repository (after installing the `dev` dependencies):

---

**NOTE**: Be aware that the tests will clear the database specified in the `DATABASE_URL` environment variable. Make sure that you are not using a database that you want to keep.

---
 
```bash
# Change to your database URL
export DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@hostname:port/dbname 
pytest
```

### Documentation

Documentation is available on [Read the Docs](https://eflips-model.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

To locally create the documentaiton from the docstrings in the code
using [sphinx-autoapi](https://sphinx-autoapi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/), you can create the documentation execute the
following command in the root directory of the repository:

```bash
sphinx-build doc/ doc/_build -W
```

### Development

We utilize the [GitHub Flow](https://docs.github.com/get-started/quickstart/github-flow) branching structure. This means  that the `main` branch is always deployable and that all development happens in feature branches. The feature branches are merged into `main` via pull requests. We utilize the [semantic versioning](https://semver.org/) scheme for versioning.

Dependencies are managed using [poetry](https://python-poetry.org/). To install the dependencies, execute the following command in the root directory of the repository:

```bash
poetry install
```

We use black for code formatting. You can use `black .` to format the code.

We use [MyPy](https://mypy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/) for static type checking. You can
use ` mypy --strict --explicit-package-bases  eflips/` to run MyPy on the code.

Please make sure that your `poetry.lock` and `pyproject.toml` files are consistent before committing. You can use `poetry check` to check this. This is also checked by pre-commit.

You can use [pre-commit](https://pre-commit.com/) to ensure that MyPy, Black, and Poetry are run before committing. To
install pre-commit, execute the following command in the root directory of the repository:

We recommend utilizing linters such as [PyLint](https://pylint.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html) for static code
analysis (but not
doing everything it says blindly).


## Usage Example

In [examples](examples/) a well-documented (german-language) [Jupyter](https://jupyter.org/) notebook can be found that explains how all pieces of the data structure fit together using the SQLAlchemy Implementation. See its [README](examples/simple_scenario_and_depot_creation_de/README.md) for details.

## License

This project is licensed under the AGPLv3 license - see the [LICENSE](LICENSE.md) file for details.

## Funding Notice

This code was developed as part of the project [eBus2030+](https://www.eflip.de/) funded by the Federal German Ministry for Digital and Transport (BMDV) under grant number 03EMF0402.

functions.name: eflips.model.migrations.env.run_migrations_offline
documentation:
Run migrations in 'offline' mode.

This configures the context with just a URL
and not an Engine, though an Engine is acceptable
here as well.  By skipping the Engine creation
we don't even need a DBAPI to be available.

Calls to context.execute() here emit the given string to the
script output.
other values from the config, defined by the needs of env.py,
can be acquired:
my_important_option = config.get_main_option("my_important_option")
... etc.

functions.name: eflips.model.migrations.env.run_migrations_online
documentation:
Run migrations in 'online' mode.

In this scenario we need to create an Engine
and associate a connection with the context.

functions.name: eflips.model.setup_database
documentation:
Use this method to create a new database from scratch.

This method will create all tables and set the alembric version to the latest version, based on this howto:
<https://alembic.sqlalchemy.org/en/latest/cookbook.html#building-an-up-to-date-database-from-scratch>

```
param engine

The engine to use to connect to the database.

return

None
```

documentation: A warning that is raised when a consistency check fails.

documentation: Utility script to import a scenario from a file.
! /usr/bin/env python3

variables.name: eflips.model.util.import.starts
documentation: type: ignore

modules.name: eflips.model.util.export
documentation: Utility script to export a scenario to a file.
! /usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: eflips.model.util.export.start_counting_foreign_keys_at
documentation:
Take a collection of related objects, and set the foreign key IDs on the local and remote side of each relationship
to values >= the given start value. This is useful when importing a scenario, as it allows to set the IDs of the
objects to be imported to values that are not already present in the database.
\:param start: A dictionary containing the starting value for each class.
\:param objs: A list of Base objects.
\:return: The same list of Base objects, but with the foreign key IDs set to values >= the given start value.

functions.name: eflips.model.util.export.get_or_update_max_sequence_number
documentation:
Loads the maximum sequence number for each table in the database. Can be used to determine the starting point for
the sequence numbers when importing a scenario. Or to fix the sequence numbers after importing a scenario.
\:param conn: An open database connection.
\:param do_update: If True, the sequence counters will also be updated in the database to continue counting after
the maximum sequence number.
\:return: A dictionary containing the maximum sequence number for each table.

functions.name: eflips.model.util.export.disconnect_obj
documentation:
Take an object and disconnect it from the database session. This will make it a pure Python object.

This is done by
\- setting the primary key to None
\- setting the object to transient state
\- setting all ids for the foreign keys to None

```
param obj

An SQLAlchemy object.

return

The same object, but disconnected from the database session.
```

functions.name: eflips.model.util.export.extract_scenario
documentation:
Extracts the scenario with the given ID from the database. It will be turned into a set of Base objects, which are
no longer attached to the database.
\:param scenario_id: The ID of the scenario to extract.
\:param session: An active database session.
\:return: A list of Base objects representing the scenario.

documentation: The station where the depot is located. This depot handles {attr}`Rotation` starting and ending at this station.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Depot.default_plan_id
documentation: The default plan of this depot. Foreign key to {attr}`Plan.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Depot.areas
documentation: The areas of this depot.

The Depot represents a palce where vehicles not engaged in a schedule are parked,
processed and dispatched.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Plan.scenario_id

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Plan.scenario

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Plan.name

The Plan represents a certain order of processes, which are executed on vehicles in a depot.

classes.name: eflips.model.depot.AreaType

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.AreaType.DIRECT_ONESIDE
documentation: A direct area where vehicles drive in form one side only.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.AreaType.DIRECT_TWOSIDE
documentation: A direct area where vehicles drive in form both sides. Also called a "herringbone" configuration.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.AreaType.LINE
documentation: A line area where vehicles are parked in a line. There might be one or more rows in the area.
This class represents the type of area in eFLIPS-Depot

classes.name: eflips.model.depot.Area

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Area.id
documentation: The unique identifier of the area. Auto-incremented.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Area.scenario_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the scenario. Foreign key to {attr}`Scenario.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Area.scenario
documentation: The scenario this area belongs to.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Area.depot_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the depot. Foreign key to {attr}`Depot.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Area.vehicle_type_id
documentation:
The unique identifier of the vehicle type. Foreign key to {attr}`VehicleType.id`.
If set, only vehicles of this type can be parked in this area. If null, all vehicle types can be parked.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Area.vehicle_type
documentation: The vehicle type which can park in this area.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Area.area_type
documentation: The type of the area. See {class}`depot.AreaType`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Area.name
documentation: An optional name for the area. If set, it must be unique within the scenario.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Area.name_short
documentation: An optional short name for the area.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Area.events
documentation: The events that happened in this area.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Area.assoc_area_processes
documentation: The association between this area and its processes.

An Area represents a certain area in a depot, where at least one process is available.

All slots in the area may be directly accessible, or the area may be a line area where vehicles are parked in a
line. In such an area, only the first vehicle in the line is directly accessible, the second vehicle can only be
removed after the first vehicle has been removed, and so on. However vehicles can only been added to the end of the
line. So if it's filled up, *all* vehicles have to be removed to add a new one. If there's one slot left and one
vehicle is removed, there's still only one slot left, not two.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Process.electric_power

The peak electric power required by this process in kW. Actual power consumption might be lower. It implies the

class_variables.name: eflips.model.depot.Process.availability

Temporal availability of this process represented by a list of start and end times. Null means this process is 
always available.
A Process represents a certain action that can be executed on a vehicle.

functions.name: eflips.model.schedule.check_trip_before_commit
documentation:
Before a trip is flushed to the database, if it has stop times:
\- Ensure that the arrival time of the first stop time is the departure time of the trip
\- Ensure that the arrival time of the last stop time is the arrival time of the trip
\- Ensure that the first stop time is the first stop of the route
\- Ensure that the last stop time is the last stop of the route

```
param target

a trip

return

Nothing. Raises an exception if something is wrong.
```

functions.name: eflips.model.schedule.check_rotation_before_commit
documentation:
A Rotation needs to be contiguous in time and space
\- the end station of a trip must be the start station of the next trip
\- the departure time of a trip must be after the arrival time of the previous trip

```
param target

A Rotation object

return

Nothing. Raises an exception if something is wrong.
```

classes.name: eflips.model.schedule.StopTime

class_variables.name: eflips.model.schedule.StopTime.id
documentation: The unique identifier of the battery type. Auto-incremented.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.schedule.StopTime.scenario_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the scenario. Foreign key to {attr}`Scenario.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.schedule.StopTime.scenario
documentation: The scenario.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.schedule.StopTime.station_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the station. Foreign key to {attr}`Station.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.schedule.StopTime.station
documentation: The station.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.schedule.StopTime.trip_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the trip. Foreign key to {attr}`Trip.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.schedule.StopTime.trip
documentation: The trip.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.schedule.StopTime.arrival_time
documentation: The arrival time at the station.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.schedule.StopTime.dwell_duration
documentation: The dwell time at the station. Defaults to 0 if unspecified.

This represents a stop time of a {class}`Trip` at a {class}`Station`.

A trip is not guaranteed to have any stop times, but it either has none (with only the arrival and departure
times set) or every stop (including the first and last) has a stop time.

documentation: = deadheading, vehicle if repositioning itself for passenger trip

class_variables.name: eflips.model.schedule.TripType.PASSENGER
documentation: Passengers may board
The type of a trip. Used in analysis to determine schedule efficiency.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.schedule.Trip.arrival_time

class_variables.name: eflips.model.schedule.Trip.trip_type

A rotation is a sequence of trips that are performed by a bus in a single day.

functions.name: eflips.model.network.check_route_before_insert_or_update
documentation:
Check the route before flushing it to the database.
\- Ensure that the distance of the first stop time is 0
\- Ensure that the distance of the last stop time is the distance of the route
\- Ensure that the first and last associated route stations correspond to the departure and arrival stations

- If we have stop times, we must ensure that the temporal order of stop times
  matches the spatial order of the stations.

```
param target



return

Nothing. Raises an exception if the route is invalid.
```

classes.name: eflips.model.network.Line

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Line.id
documentation: The unique identifier of the battery type. Auto-incremented.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Line.scenario_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the scenario. Foreign key to {attr}`Scenario.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Line.scenario
documentation: The scenario.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Line.name
documentation:
The name of the line. Usually a number or letter, e.g. "1" or "A" followed by the terminal stations.
Example: "1 - Hauptbahnhof <-> Hauptfriedhof"

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Line.name_short
documentation: The short name of the line. Usually a number or letter, e.g. "1" or "A".

The Line represents a bus line, which is a collection of {class}`Route` that belong together. This may not
include all routes that a bus on this line takes over its service day, since depot and deadhead routes are not
included (they can be shared between multiple lines).

classes.name: eflips.model.network.Route

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.id
documentation: The unique identifier of the battery type. Auto-incremented.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.scenario_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the scenario. Foreign key to {attr}`Scenario.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.scenario
documentation: The scenario.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.departure_station_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the departure station. Foreign key to {attr}`Station.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.departure_station
documentation: The departure station.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.arrival_station_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the arrival station. Foreign key to {attr}`Station.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.arrival_station
documentation: The arrival station.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.line_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the line. Foreign key to {attr}`Line.id`. May be `None`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.line
documentation: The line.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.name_short
documentation: The short name of the route (if available).

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.headsign
documentation: The headsign of the route (if available). This is whatever is displayed on the bus.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.distance
documentation: The length of the route in meters.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.geom
documentation:
The shape of the route as a polyline. If set, the length of this shape must be equal to {attr}`Route.distance`.
Use WGS84 coordinates (EPSG:4326).

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.trips
documentation: The trips.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.assoc_route_stations
documentation: The associated route stations. This contains metadata about the stops on the route, such as the partial distance.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Route.stations
documentation: This is a list of all stations on the route.
A route is a fixed geometry that a bus takes. It is a part of a {class}`Line`. A trip takes place on a route.

classes.name: eflips.model.network.VoltageLevel

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.VoltageLevel.HV
documentation: High Voltage, e.g. 110kV transmission grid

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.VoltageLevel.HV_MV
documentation: Both high and medium voltage

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.VoltageLevel.MV
documentation: Medium Voltage, e.g. 10kV distribution grid

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.VoltageLevel.MV_LV
documentation: Both medium and low voltage

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.VoltageLevel.LV
documentation: Low voltage, e.g. 400V three- phase
The voltage level of a charging infrastructure. Used in analysis and simulation to determine grid load.

classes.name: eflips.model.network.ChargeType

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.ChargeType.depb
documentation: Only charge when vehicle is not on a rotation

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.ChargeType.oppb
documentation: Aka „terminus charging“. While on a rotation, charge in the breaks between trips

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.ChargeType.DEPOT
documentation: Legacy value for depb

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.ChargeType.OPPORTUNITY
documentation: Legacy value for oppb

The type of charging infrastructure. Only vehicle types with opportunity charging can charge at opportunity
charging stations.

classes.name: eflips.model.network.Station

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.id
documentation: The unique identifier of the battery type. Auto-incremented.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.scenario_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the scenario. Foreign key to {attr}`Scenario.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.scenario
documentation: The scenario.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.name
documentation: The name of the station. Example: "Hauptbahnhof"

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.name_short
documentation: The short name of the station (if available).

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.geom
documentation: The (optional) location of the station as a point. Use WGS84 coordinates (EPSG:4326).

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.is_electrified
documentation:
Whether the station has a charging infrastructure. If yes, then

- `amount_charging_places` must be set
- `power_per_charger` must be set
- `power_total` must be set
- `charge_type` must be set
- `voltage_level` must be set

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.amount_charging_places
documentation: The amount of charging poles at the station. If `is_electrified` is true, this must be set.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.power_per_charger
documentation: The power per charger in kW. If `is_electrified` is true, this must be set.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.power_total
documentation: The total power of the charging infrastructure in kW. If `is_electrified` is true, this must be set.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.charge_type
documentation:
The type of charging infrastructure. If `is_electrified` is true, this must be set. 

When running simBA and eflips, this is set to `oppb` for all stations. `depb` only makes sense in standalone
simBA runs.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.voltage_level
documentation: The voltage level of the charging infrastructure. If `is_electrified` is true, this must be set.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.depot
documentation: The (optional) depot that is associated with this station. Only set if the station has a depot.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.stop_times
documentation: The stop times.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.trips
documentation: The trips stopping at this station.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.assoc_route_stations
documentation: The associated route stations. This contains metadata about the stops on the route, such as the partial distance.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.routes
documentation: This is a list of all routes that stop at this station.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.Station.events
documentation: The events that take place at this station. Only expected to be filled for charging at electrified stations.

A Station is a point on the map that a bus can stop at. It is visited on a {class}`Trip`.
The station may not mark the exact location of the stop, but rather a point nearby, in case the station is part of a
larger complex.

classes.name: eflips.model.network.AssocRouteStation

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.AssocRouteStation.id
documentation: The unique identifier of the association. Auto-incremented.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.AssocRouteStation.scenario_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the scenario. Foreign key to {attr}`Scenario.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.AssocRouteStation.route_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the route. Foreign key to {attr}`Route.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.AssocRouteStation.route
documentation: The route.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.AssocRouteStation.station_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the station. Foreign key to {attr}`Station.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.AssocRouteStation.station
documentation: The station.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.AssocRouteStation.location
documentation: An optional precise location of the this route's stop at the station. Use WGS84 coordinates (EPSG:4326).

class_variables.name: eflips.model.network.AssocRouteStation.elapsed_distance
documentation: The distance in m that the bus has traveled when it reached this stop.
An association table between {class}`Route` and {class}`Station`. It is used to represent the stops on a route.

functions.name: eflips.model.general.check_vehicle_type_before_commit
documentation:
A VehicleType may hav consumption xor consumption_lut, but not both.

```
param target

A VehicleType object

return

Nothing. Raises an exception if something is wrong.
```

methods.name: eflips.model.general.Scenario._copy_object
documentation:
Internal helper function to copy an SQLAlchemy object and attach it to a new scenario.
\:param obj: An SQLAlchemy object. Must have an 'id' attribute and a 'scenario' attribute.
\:param session: an SQLAlchemy session.
\:return: The new object, attached to the new scenario.

methods.name: eflips.model.general.Scenario.clone
documentation:
Creates a copy of the scenario, including all vehicle types and battery types.
\:param session: The database session.
\:return: The copy of the scenario.

methods.name: eflips.model.general.Scenario.select_rotations
documentation:
Keeps only the rotations that are within the time window. Deletes all other rotations from the database. This
method is useful if (for example) your import gave you a six-month schedule, but you only want to simulate a
week of it.

```
param session

An SQLAlchemy session to a database with eflips-model tables.

param start_time

The start time of the time window. Rotations that start before this time are not selected.
This time must have a timezone.

param time_window

The time window. Rotations that end after this time are not selected.

return

None
```

classes.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.id
documentation: The unique identifier of the vehicle type. Auto-incremented.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.scenario_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the scenario. Foreign key to {attr}`Scenario.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.scenario
documentation: The scenario.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.battery_type_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the battery type. Foreign key to {attr}`BatteryType.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.battery_type
documentation: The battery type.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.name
documentation: A name for the vehicle type.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.name_short
documentation: An optional short name for the vehicle type.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.battery_capacity
documentation: The battery capacity in kWh. This refers to the usable capacity, not the total capacity.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.battery_capacity_reserve
documentation:
The battery capacity reserve below 0 kWh 'capacity' in kWh. Using this value in generating evaluation, 
things such as "always 10% reserve" can be modeled.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.charging_curve
documentation:
The charging curve of the vehicle type. This is a 2D array of floats with two rows. The first row contains
the state of charge, ranging from 0 (or some negative value if there is a nonzero reserve) to 1. The second row
contains the charging power in kW. The charging curve is used to calculate the charging power of a vehicle
using linear interpolation. The charging curve must be monotonically increasing in the first row.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.v2g_curve
documentation:
The vehicle-to-grid curve of the vehicle type. This is a 2D array of floats with two rows. The first row contains
the state of charge, ranging from 0 to 1. The second row contains the discharging power in kW. The v2g curve is
used to calculate the discharging power of a vehicle using linear interpolation. The v2g curve must be monotonically
increasing in the first row. It may bo None if the vehicle type does not support vehicle-to-grid.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.charging_efficiency
documentation: Ratio of battery output (while driving/V2G) to grid input. Also applies to V2G.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.opportunity_charging_capable
documentation:
Whether the bus is capable of automatic highpower charging. All buses are assumed to be capable of (depot) 
conductive charging.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.minimum_charging_power
documentation: If the charging power falls below this value, charging is canceled

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.length
documentation: The length of the vehicle in meters.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.width
documentation: The width of the vehicle in meters.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.height
documentation: The height of the vehicle in meters.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.empty_mass
documentation: The empty mass of the vehicle in kg.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.allowed_mass
documentation: The allowed payload mass of the vehicle in kg. The total mass of the vehicle is empty_mass + allowed_mass.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.consumption
documentation:
The vehicle's energy consumption in kWh/km. This is used to calculate the energy consumption of a trip. Can
be None if we are using more detailed consumption models.

Either this or consumption_lut must be specified. Both cannot exist at the same time.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.vehicles
documentation: A list of vehicles.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.vehicle_classes

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.rotations
documentation: A list of rotations.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.events
documentation: A list of events.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleType.areas
documentation: A list of areas.

This class represents a vehicle type, containing the technical parameters shared by all vehicles of this type.
It is used by vehicles (which are of a specific type) and by the rotations (which are for specific vehicle types).

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Vehicle.name_short

classes.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleClass

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleClass.id
documentation: The unique identifier of the battery type. Auto-incremented.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleClass.scenario_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the scenario. Foreign key to {attr}`Scenario.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleClass.scenario
documentation: The scenario.

documentation: A name for the vehicle class.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleClass.name_short
documentation: An optional short name for the vehicle class.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.VehicleClass.consumption_lut
documentation:
A consumption look up table.

Either this or consumption must be specified. Both cannot exist at the same time.

VehicleClasses allow a many-to-many relationship between vehicles and classes, which may be used for specifying
things such as "any 12m bus" or "any 18m bus".

The VehicleClass table is not used directly, but through the association table AssocVehicleTypeVehicleClass.

**Support is currently incomplete. THis only exists as a stub (and is used by eflip-LCA), but is not implemented
in django.simba or eflips-depot**

classes.name: eflips.model.general.AssocVehicleTypeVehicleClass

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.AssocVehicleTypeVehicleClass.id
documentation: Not the primary key and not used in SQLAlchemy, but required by Django.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.AssocVehicleTypeVehicleClass.vehicle_type_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the vehicle type. Foreign key to {attr}`VehicleType.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.AssocVehicleTypeVehicleClass.vehicle_class_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the vehicle class. Foreign key to {attr}`VehicleClass.id`.
The association table for the many-to-many relationship between vehicles and classes.

classes.name: eflips.model.general.EventType

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.EventType.DRIVING
documentation: Driving on a trip.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.EventType.CHARGING_OPPORTUNITY
documentation: Charging at a terminal station.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.EventType.CHARGING_DEPOT
documentation: Charging at a depot.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.EventType.SERVICE
documentation: Service at a depot. Probably, the description field should be used to specify the type of service.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.EventType.STANDBY
documentation: Standing in the depot while waiting for something. Not yet ready for departure.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.EventType.STANDBY_DEPARTURE
documentation: Ready for departure from a depot.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.EventType.PRECONDITIONING
documentation: HVAC is turned on using grid power.

The EventType can be used to filter for certain types of events. It is also used to determine the valid combinations
of nullable fields in the Event table.

classes.name: eflips.model.general.Event

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.id
documentation: The unique identifier of the event. Auto-incremented.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.scenario_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the scenario. Foreign key to {attr}`Scenario.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.scenario
documentation: The scenario.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.vehicle_type_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the vehicle type. Foreign key to {attr}`VehicleType.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.vehicle_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the vehicle. Foreign key to {attr}`Vehicle.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.vehicle
documentation: The vehicle.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.station_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the station. Foreign key to {attr}`Station.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.area_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the area in the depot. Foreign key to {attr}`Area.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.subloc_no
documentation:
The number of the sub-location in the depot or multi-chargpoint terminal. The mapping of sub-locations to
physical locations is defined by the depot layout and/or the multi-chargpoint terminal layout.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.trip_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the trip. Foreign key to {attr}`Trip.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.time_start
documentation: The time the event starts.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.time_end
documentation: The time the event ends.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.soc_start
documentation: The state of charge at the start of the event. This should refer to the net battery capacity.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.soc_end
documentation: The state of charge at the end of the event. This should refer to the net battery capacity.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.event_type
documentation: The type of the event.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.description
documentation: A description of the event. Used to display additional information to the user.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Event.timeseries
documentation:
Dict with mandatory keys „time“ (ISO 18601 with TZ), „soc“ (0-1) and optional keys „distance“ (m, along route for 
trip) + other freely defined keys. Array of same length for each key

An Event represents a signle event in the simulation. This does not necessary mean a point in time, but a process
during which something happens. For example, there are charging and driving events. Events are used to track the
state of the simulation. They are also the basis for the evaluation of the simulation.

Note that there are only certain valid combinations of the nullable fields.
An event can take place either at a
- station (station_id is not null and subloc_no is not null). Possible events: CHARGING_OPPORTUNITY
- depot (station_id is not null and subloc_no is null). Possible events: CHARGING_DEPOT, SERVICE, STANDBY_DEPARTURE
PRECONDITIONING
- trip (trip_id is not null and subloc_no is null). Possible events: DRIVING

classes.name: eflips.model.general.ConsumptionLut

methods.name: eflips.model.general.ConsumptionLut.calc_consumption
documentation:
This function calculates the consumption of the trip according to the model of
Ji, Bie, Zeng, Wang <https://doi.org/10.1016/j.commtr.2022.100069>
\:param trip_distance: Travelled distance in km
\:param temperature: Average temperature during trip in degrees Celsius
\:param mass: Curb weight + passengers in kg
\:param duration: Trip time in minutes
\:return: Trip energy in kWh

methods.name: eflips.model.general.ConsumptionLut.table_generator
documentation:
Takes VehicleType information to create a consumption table in django-simba format.
:return:

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.ConsumptionLut.id
documentation: The unique identifier of the consumption. Auto-incremented.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.ConsumptionLut.scenario_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the scenario. Foreign key to {attr}`Scenario.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.ConsumptionLut.scenario
documentation: The scenario.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.ConsumptionLut.name
documentation: A name for the consumption table.
Because django-simba requires a name

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.ConsumptionLut.vehicle_class_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the vehicle type. Foreign key to {attr}`VehicleClass.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.ConsumptionLut.vehicle_class
documentation: The vehicle class.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.ConsumptionLut.columns
documentation:
A JSON-encoded list of column name strings. The order of these should match the order of the values for each row
in the data_points

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.ConsumptionLut.data_points
documentation:
A list of data points. These are the coordinates of the data point. Its value is stored in the `value` column.
The order of columns is the entry in the `columns` column.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.ConsumptionLut.values
documentation:
A list of consumption values in kWh/km. The corresponding temperatures, inclines etc. are stored in the 
`data_points` column.

The Consumption table stores the energy consumption look-up-tables for each vehicle class.

Uses a regression model generated from real world electric bus data to create a consumption table in
django-simba format (temperature, speed, level of loading, incline, consumption) and exports it into
the session database.

classes.name: eflips.model.general.Temperatures

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Temperatures.id
documentation: The unique identifier of the consumption. Auto-incremented.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Temperatures.scenario_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the scenario. Foreign key to {attr}`Scenario.id`.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Temperatures.scenario
documentation: The scenario.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Temperatures.name
documentation: A name for the temperature table.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Temperatures.use_only_time
documentation:
Whether the temperature data is for one repeating day. If False, the temperature data is for multiple days.
If true, it is for one day and all other days should use the values from this day.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Temperatures.datetimes
documentation:
The datetimes of the temperature data. If is_one_repeating_day is True, this should be a single day.
The length of this list should be the same as the length of the temperatures.

class_variables.name: eflips.model.general.Temperatures.data
documentation:
The temperatures in degrees Celsius. The order of the temperatures should match the order of the datetimes.
The length of this list should be the same as the length of the datetimes.

The Consumption table stores the energy consumption look-up-tables for each vehicle class.

Uses a regression model generated from real world electric bus data to create a consumption table in
django-simba format (temperature, speed, level of loading, incline, consumption) and exports it into
the session database.
